title: The Gang Does a Clip Show

jokes.text: What are we gonna do without our phones for 30 minutes?
jokes.setup: Characters face a 30-minute phone update
jokes.punchline: Modern addiction to phones makes 30 minutes seem impossible
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about phone addiction, well-delivered with appropriate character panic

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What are we gonna do without our phones for 30 minutes?

jokes.text: That's what you do when you start getting old. You know, you start reliving the glory days 'cause you can't think of anything new to do.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to reminisce about old times
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately explains why this is pathetic aging behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis character work - immediately turns nostalgic moment into superiority complex analysis

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's what you do when you start getting old. You know, you start reliving the glory days 'cause you can't think of anything new to do.

jokes.text: Do you remember the time that I sewed myself into the couch naked?
jokes.setup: Suggestion to remember good old times
jokes.punchline: Charlie's idea of a good memory is bizarre self-harm
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Charlie moment - the casual delivery of something horrifying is classic Sunny

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Do you remember the time that I sewed myself into the couch naked?

jokes.text: Oh, Jesus Christ! Oh, God! Oh, God!
jokes.setup: Frank mentions sewing himself naked into couch
jokes.punchline: Everyone's horrified reaction to the memory
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction beat that sells Frank's disturbing story

dialogue.line: Oh, Jesus Christ! Oh, God! Oh, God!

jokes.text: Frank bursting out of couch naked, sweating, gasping for air
jokes.setup: Charlie mentions sewing himself into couch
jokes.punchline: Visual of Frank violently emerging from couch like alien birth
jokes.explanation: Great visual payoff to Charlie's setup - Frank's emergence is both disgusting and hilarious

dialogue.line: Frank bursting out of couch naked, sweating, gasping for air

jokes.text: Uh, hola, estúpidos.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to contribute a memory
jokes.punchline: Bad Spanish accent as 'hilarious character'
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Mac's delusional belief in his comedic abilities

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Uh, hola, estúpidos.

jokes.text: You're remembering the time you burned a Mexican's house down?
jokes.setup: Mac doing bad Spanish accent
jokes.punchline: Dennis assumes it's referencing arson
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation typical of Sunny - casual mention of hate crime arson

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're remembering the time you burned a Mexican's house down?

jokes.text: Taiwan Tammy
jokes.setup: Mac insisting he has hilarious characters
jokes.punchline: Racist character name that others immediately reject
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor about Mac's offensive 'characters' being shut down by group

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Taiwan Tammy

jokes.text: Why don't we remember all the times that Dee's gotten hurt.
jokes.setup: Looking for new memory category
jokes.punchline: Gleeful suggestion to relive Dee's physical pain
jokes.explanation: Sets up montage while showing Mac's sadistic joy in Dee's suffering

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Why don't we remember all the times that Dee's gotten hurt.

jokes.text: Dee falling through ceiling finding kittens
jokes.setup: Mac's suggestion to remember Dee getting hurt
jokes.punchline: Dee crashes through ceiling while trying to rescue kittens
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with ironic twist - gets hurt while doing something good

dialogue.line: Dee falling through ceiling finding kittens

jokes.text: You guys are exaggerating. You weren't even there for half of that. It's just how we remember it, Dee.
jokes.setup: Dee protesting the accuracy of her injury montage
jokes.punchline: Mac admitting they weren't there but that's their memory
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on memory distortion and clip show conventions

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You guys are exaggerating. You weren't even there for half of that.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's just how we remember it, Dee.

jokes.text: Repeated 'Did you fuck my mom' escalation
jokes.setup: Child already asked once
jokes.punchline: Relentless repetition with increasing aggression
jokes.explanation: Good escalation technique, though less impactful than initial shock

dialogue.line: Repeated 'Did you fuck my mom' escalation

jokes.text: Because I cut the brakes. Wild card, bitches!
jokes.setup: Car won't stop
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually reveals he sabotaged the brakes
jokes.explanation: Iconic Charlie moment - chaotic energy with perfect delivery, became cultural reference

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Because I cut the brakes. Wild card, bitches!

jokes.text: I have a touch of consumption.
jokes.setup: Frank coughing dramatically
jokes.punchline: Casually claims to have 19th century disease
jokes.explanation: Absurd Frank moment with perfect deadpan delivery of outdated medical term

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I have a touch of consumption.

jokes.text: The implication that things might go wrong for her if she refuses to sleep with me.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his boat plan
jokes.punchline: Casual description of sexual coercion
jokes.explanation: Infamous Dennis sociopathy moment that became cultural touchstone for predatory behavior

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The implication that things might go wrong for her if she refuses to sleep with me.

jokes.text: I don't feel we're reading as eagles. No, bro. We look like assholes.
jokes.setup: Attempting to look like eagles
jokes.punchline: Complete failure acknowledged
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback with self-aware failure acknowledgment

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I don't feel we're reading as eagles.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, bro. We look like assholes.

jokes.text: Hello, fellow American. This you should vote me. I leave power. Good.
jokes.setup: Charlie reading prepared speech
jokes.punchline: Completely incoherent grammar and syntax
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie illiteracy humor with absurd political speech structure

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Hello, fellow American. This you should vote me. I leave power. Good.

jokes.text: Well, I'm gonna... I'm gonna watch you do it.
jokes.setup: Mac's suicide plan needing Charlie's participation
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually opts to just observe instead
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual abandonment of Mac's dramatic plan perfectly subverts expectation

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, I'm gonna... I'm gonna watch you do it.

jokes.text: Why didn't you jump out?
jokes.setup: Mac crashes car into wall
jokes.punchline: Charlie asks obvious question about Mac not escaping
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist beat highlighting the stupidity of the situation

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Why didn't you jump out?

jokes.text: I am not an ostrich.
jokes.setup: Memory sequence ending
jokes.punchline: Dee's defensive reaction to bird comparison
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to running gag with Dee's frustrated defensiveness

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I am not an ostrich.

jokes.text: I quit the bar, I moved to North Dakota
jokes.setup: Conflicting memories about who turned off lights
jokes.punchline: Dennis insisting on his dramatic exit that others forgot
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about actual show events with Dennis's narcissistic need for recognition

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I quit the bar, I moved to North Dakota

jokes.text: It's like fake news, you know? And then, all of a sudden, nobody knows what the truth is, and facts don't matter.
jokes.setup: Memories being distorted
jokes.punchline: Dennis making surprisingly coherent political commentary
jokes.explanation: Decent topical humor with Dennis accidentally making sense

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's like fake news, you know? And then, all of a sudden, nobody knows what the truth is, and facts don't matter.

jokes.text: They gave me a magic pill, and I learned it, and I speak it fluently.
jokes.setup: Charlie claiming to have spoken Chinese
jokes.punchline: Believes he learned language via magic pill
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie delusion with perfect confidence in impossible explanation

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: They gave me a magic pill, and I learned it, and I speak it fluently.

jokes.text: What's happening? montage
jokes.setup: Charlie's suggestion to remember confusion
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire montage of characters asking 'what's happening'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating montage that highlights running theme of group confusion

dialogue.line: What's happening?

jokes.text: Remembering Seinfeld episode as their own memory
jokes.setup: Discussing 'the contest'
jokes.punchline: They're remembering a famous Seinfeld episode as if they lived it
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about false memories, good misdirection technique

jokes.text: Seinfeld 'The Contest' scene recreation
jokes.setup: Remembering 'their' contest
jokes.punchline: Actually remembering Seinfeld episode verbatim
jokes.explanation: Clever meta humor about false memories and television influence

jokes.text: We had our own contest, except ours was to see who could masturbate the most in a 24-hour period.
jokes.setup: Realizing they remembered Seinfeld, not their own experience
jokes.punchline: Their actual contest was much more disturbing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sunny escalation - taking wholesome Seinfeld concept to disgusting extreme

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We had our own contest, except ours was to see who could masturbate the most in a 24-hour period.

jokes.text: The skin has been all but removed from the organs.
jokes.setup: Hospital visit from masturbation contest
jokes.punchline: Graphic medical description of self-inflicted damage
jokes.explanation: Disturbing payoff to setup with clinical description adding to absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: The skin has been all but removed from the organs.

jokes.text: The night we had sex. That was... that was a great night. And I'm pregnant.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to alter reality through memory
jokes.punchline: Fantasy scenario with the waitress
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's delusions about his relationship

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The night we had sex. That was... that was a great night.

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: And I'm pregnant.

jokes.text: Charlie's false memory of being with the waitress and having a baby
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to alter reality through memory
jokes.punchline: Creates elaborate fantasy of relationship success
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's delusions and wishful thinking

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie's false memory of being with the waitress and having a baby

jokes.text: Two Charlies with different voice pitches arguing
jokes.setup: Charlie's memory creates duplicate
jokes.punchline: Two Charlies with different voices arguing about normalcy
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist concept with good execution of voice/identity confusion

jokes.text: Frank, why do you have hair? My legs have always been long. It's a burden being tall.
jokes.setup: Realizing they're in someone's distorted memory
jokes.punchline: Frank imagines himself as tall and hairy
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag concept with Frank's delusional self-image

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Frank, why do you have hair?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: My legs have always been long. It's a burden being tall.

jokes.text: It's a burden being tall
jokes.setup: Frank claiming to have long legs
jokes.punchline: Frank seriously discussing burden of being tall when he's obviously short
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment with Frank's complete lack of self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's a burden being tall

jokes.text: He spends three hours a day on a dildo bike.
jokes.setup: Dennis forced to admit why he dislikes living with Mac
jokes.punchline: Specific, humiliating detail about Mac's exercise routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect combination of specific detail and character revelation with great comedic timing

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He spends three hours a day on a dildo bike.

jokes.text: Inception top spinning indefinitely
jokes.setup: Using Inception reference to test reality
jokes.punchline: Top spins forever, suggesting they're still not in reality
jokes.explanation: Good meta reference with appropriate ambiguous ending for the concept
